Hey guys, today we're going to talk about how to get you and prevent you from going to Facebook jail. So Facebook is very picky on the advertising policies and so I want to make sure that everything that you guys are doing keeps showing up and up so your account doesn't get shut down and your regeneration does not stop. Now just note going into this, I'm going to give you the guidelines. It's the Pirates of the Caribbean, they're rules but kind of guidelines because Facebook has a couple different processes they do to review this. They have an automated process where it's just software and code doing it but they also have third party people to go through so sometimes you will get lied for things that are totally off the wall, sometimes they'll be really strict and there's a lot of things that you might get an ad approved with one of the things I want to talk to you about and then six months later it might get shut down and you might get in trouble so it's always important to try to follow these guidelines as much as possible but know that there is inconsistencies in all of this. So one of the first ones is as you first start doing your ad account, I've seen this happen a couple of times, if you're logging in from multiple computers especially in a short amount of time that can show some inconsistency they might think your account's being hacked. I wouldn't stress this one too much but just know if you're traveling just be aware of it, you can use your phone to add manager to log this in as a security code generator, not a big deal. Your landing page has to deliver what's promised in the ad copy. So for instance if your ad copy is going to promise a free ebook it needs to be on the landing page and the copy say has to be the same thing. This is a huge thing we've got to be consistent with. Jim Launch we should hopefully be providing you with a lot of the stuff that will help with this but just be aware of it. Your landing page needs to offer one thing. So you can't have all certain different buttons, different places to go that will just confuse it Facebook will shut you down. This is probably the biggest new one that's happened to a lot of people is your landing page needs to have trusted elements. Now you may have one up live right now that doesn't have that but you're going to need your terms and conditions, your privacy policy which Jim Launch provides you with templates for. Also just your address, your website, all these things just showing that this is a valid website and it needs to be on all the pages not just the initial one. You cannot promise something specific so it's like hey lose six pounds in six days. That is where you will get in trouble. You can offer generalized things like hey healthy tips to lose weight but again do this to lose weight in three days we'll get you in trouble. So be aware of the specificity we will help you with some of these things as well. You can't call out people you can't make them feel insecure about themselves like are you fat here's how to be skinny. So instead of talking about the user focus on what you're offering and what they're going to get free weight loss guide scrumptious lunchman plans things like that way better than being specific towards them. A lot of times even just saying do you sometimes we'll get you flat. Any ads that promote the ideal body now this is more saying you should look this way do this but this is a gray area as well because sometimes you will literally just get flagged from an image like this that will say hey you're promoting an ideal body image that isn't real and so just be aware of that but this is kind of a tough one to do and you can't say anything in the copy like you should look this way you should do this. Using copyrighted material now gyms we have music playing and a lot of times people put videos up with their gym people doing squats doing this and they'll have music in the background that's copyrighted and that actually can get your account flagged so be aware of that using something like in shot to put non copyrighted material or music over that will be a great way to save you from getting in trouble with this. This will also prevent you from posting video for a while too which is a pain in the butt so do not use copyrighted material. Know before and after this is on your landing pages this is on your ads this is anywhere it can't even like I've seen before and afters of houses on marketing that got flagged because they just do not want before and after so be very aware of that you can get flagged instantaneously for that so be aware. You cannot focus on one body part so an image of this person's ads would be too specific on their stomach now you hand out a little bit and have the whole body probably get away with it but that particular picture of just their stomach will get you in trouble. No images of people measuring themselves so they can't be tape measuring scales things like that these will all get you flagged and that's really really easy to do because it seems harmless but this will get you in trouble so nothing of people measuring themselves sometimes even looking in the mirror will get you in trouble it's just kind of weird. This is probably the one you will hit the most too sexy or suggestive now this is going to be up to the person who's reviewing your ads. I personally had a tank top on one time I told it was too suggestive so for instance there are ways around this so the woman on the left here in the blue underpants would probably get you flagged. The person on the right in the grey pants probably would just because there's more clothing. Same thing here on the two girls in bikini the one of the butts probably will get you shut down versus the one on the bottom. I'm sorry one on the bottom probably might get away with it it's a crapshoot and this is where I will say that sometimes Facebook it just depends on who is working that day so be aware of that and just think if you have to ask yourself like would this get shut down it probably will. Just know that we will always be trying to test that to help you out on this but it is a very grey area and it just depends on who is on that day. So quick summary of all this these are all guidelines but they're grey and it depends on who is reviewing it. A couple tips I would suggest always having a second ad account ready to go so your business is not stopped if this one gets shut down and continue to just play with it. The generation is the lifeblood of your business you need to be smart and to be safe.
